name: Puig Castellar and Besòs River Loop
name_original: Santa Coloma de Gramenet a Poble Iberico con vuelta en el camino de colesterol de Besos
description: This 7.6-mile mountain biking loop offers an easy ride along the Besòs River and a climb to Puig Castellar for panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet offer diverse landscapes, from the revitalized Besòs River banks to the elevated Puig Castellar. The region provides access to larger natural parks like Serra de Collserola and Parc de la Serralada de Marina, which feature extensive trail networks. This varied terrain includes river paths, hills, and forested areas, catering to different mountain biking preferences. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet The most popular mountain bike trail is Puig Castellar and Besòs River Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Besòs River and includes a climb to Puig Castellar, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Fondo (L9), a difficult 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and is suitable for experienced riders seeking a challenging ride to the La Coscollada Watchtower. Local mountain bikers also love the Camí dels Avellaners – Canaletes Aqueduct loop from Santa Rosa, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Coloma De Gramenet is defined by river paths, hills, and access to natural park trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides along the river to challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Santa Coloma De Gramenet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1,500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Coloma De Gramenet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: There are 18 mountain bike trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet listed on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy rides along the river to challenging climbs in the hills.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 5 easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939969610/puig-castellar-and-besos-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar and Besòs River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.3 km long and follows the revitalized Besòs River, including a climb to Puig Castellar for panoramic views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in this region?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santa Coloma De Gramenet offer diverse terrain. You can expect river paths along the Besòs River, hills with significant elevation changes, and forested areas. The region also provides access to larger natural parks like Serra de Collserola and Parc de la Serralada de Marina, which feature extensive trail networks with varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. There are 11 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16816820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Fondo (L9)&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.3 km path with significant elevation gains. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32587382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí dels Avellaners – Canaletes Aqueduct loop from Santa Rosa&lt;/a&gt;, covering 34.9 km through varied terrain.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while mountain biking in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: Many trails offer views of interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939969610/puig-castellar-and-besos-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar and Besòs River Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes, like the Lion Fountain loops, lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, offering great views. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Les Maleses&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montigalá Cross&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santa Coloma De Gramenet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river paths to challenging hill climbs, and the accessibility to natural parks. Over 1,500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the area's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that form a loop?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Santa Coloma De Gramenet are loops. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939969610/puig-castellar-and-besos-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar and Besòs River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32018374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Església Major&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32628587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Zam Park – Canaletes Fields loop from Can Zam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: Santa Coloma De Gramenet, being near Barcelona, generally offers pleasant weather for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures, making them ideal. Even winter can be suitable, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific regulations for each trail can vary, many natural areas in Catalonia, including those accessible from Santa Coloma De Gramenet, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on individual trails.

Q: Is there public transport access to the mountain bike trails?
A: Santa Coloma De Gramenet is well-connected by public transport, particularly with Barcelona's metro system (L9 and L1 lines). Many trailheads, especially those closer to urban areas or parks like Can Zam, are often accessible via public transport. For routes starting further afield, a combination of public transport and a short ride might be necessary.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Given Santa Coloma De Gramenet's urban setting and proximity to various parks and the Besòs River, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops within easy reach of many trailheads and along the riverbanks. Areas around Parc de Can Zam or the city center will offer plenty of options for refreshments before or after your ride.

Q: Do I need a permit to mountain bike in Santa Coloma De Gramenet or nearby natural parks?
A: Generally, you do not need a specific permit for mountain biking on designated trails in Santa Coloma De Gramenet or the surrounding natural parks like Serra de Collserola. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of other trail users and the environment.
tips: Watchtower to prevent fires. Good area for hiking and biking. The views from here are spectacular.

The Iberian settlement of Puig Castellar is an Iberian settlement located in Santa Coloma de Gramenet, at the top of Puig Castellar, 303 m. It was founded by the Laietan tribe around the 6th century BC and was abandoned between the 3rd and 2nd centuries BC. It is worth noting the old part, sunny, and the new part, in the shade. It is one of the best preserved Iberian settlements around the city of Barcelona.

Path to ride on MTB or gravel that runs parallel to the bed of the Besós river in its lower part. You can start riding from the mouth of the river in Sant Adrià de Besòs where it begins with paved bike lanes to the Can Zam Park in Santa Coloma de Gramenet where this dirt path begins right away. At this point there is the possibility of going up to the upper part of the river where there are paved bike lanes up to Montcada i Reixac.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This loop combines two very different areas. While the Besòs River section is generally flat and easy, the ascent towards Puig Castellar involves rugged terrain, steep climbs, and technical sections. The full loop is considered a hard mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. Beginners might find the Puig Castellar portion challenging and may need to push their bikes in some sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a dramatic contrast. The Besòs River section features mostly paved paths with some dirt and gravel, ideal for casual cycling. As you head towards Puig Castellar, the terrain becomes much more rugged, with hard climbs, technical descents, roots, and rock gardens, typical of the Serralada Marina area.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied scenery. Along the Besòs River, you'll enjoy urban river landscapes, green spaces, and a cool ecosystem popular for migratory birds. As you climb towards Puig Castellar, you'll find the historical Iberian Settlement of Puig Castellar and magnificent panoramic views of Barcelona and the coastline from the View of Barcelona from Poblat Ibèric Watchtower.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially on the exposed climbs to Puig Castellar, while winter can bring cooler, potentially wet conditions. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: Parking options are available near the Besòs River Park in Santa Coloma de Gramenet, which is a good starting point for the loop. Look for street parking or designated parking areas in the vicinity of the river park or near Can Zam.

faqs.question: Is this loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trails, but they must be kept on a leash, especially in populated areas along the Besòs River and within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to ride this loop. Both the Besòs River Park and the trails leading to Puig Castellar within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. Along the Besòs River, you'll ride parts of the Ruta Mediterrània, La Jonquera - Sant Adrià del Besós, Ronda Verda - Sector Besòs, and Bicivia 8. As you head towards Puig Castellar, you might also encounter segments of the GR 92 BTT and trails like De la font de l'Alzina al Puig Castellar.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and difficulty, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a helmet, appropriate mountain biking gear, a repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Sun protection is also advisable, especially on exposed sections.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the Besòs River section, especially closer to urban areas, you'll find cafes and public restrooms. However, as you ascend towards Puig Castellar, facilities become scarce, so plan accordingly and make use of amenities before heading into the more remote hilly sections.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking in Parc de la Serralada de Marina?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in Parc de la Serralada de Marina, which encompasses Puig Castellar, it's important to stay on marked trails, respect wildlife, and avoid disturbing the natural environment. Keep your speed in check, especially on descents, and be mindful of hikers and other trail users. Dogs must be on a leash.
